SECTION 1  
My answer is yes.  
The task requires developing a Python function using the google-cloud-bigtable client library to extract data from Google Cloud Bigtable. This involves setting up the client, connecting to the instance, and retrieving data from a specified table. The next step is to perform advanced anomaly detection on the extracted data. This can be achieved by preprocessing the data, applying statistical methods, and using machine learning models to identify outliers or unusual patterns. The function should be structured to handle data retrieval and anomaly detection independently, ensuring that each module is self-contained and focused on its specific aspect. The response must start and end with the same word, and all words with all capital letters should appear less than three times. The total number of sentences must be less than 36.
